Estadio Azteca becomes the first stadium to host matches at three World Cups
articles.summary: Mexico's opening 2-0 win over South Africa on June 11 made the Azteca the first venue in history to stage matches at three separate World Cups — 1970, 1986, and now 2026. Few grounds in world football carry more weight, and the opening match lived up to it.

articles.headline: Tifos, terrace culture, and the fan sections to watch all tournament
articles.summary: The American Outlaws, Argentina's La Doce, the Dutch Orange army, and Moroccan and Egyptian ultras are bringing some of the most elaborate visual displays ever seen at a World Cup in North America. The atmosphere around these matches is part of the show.

articles.headline: Meet 'Stella', the official 2026 World Cup match ball
articles.summary: Adidas's 'Stella' is the official ball of the 2026 FIFA World Cup — thermally bonded and AI-aerodynamics tested. Available in official and replica versions ($175 / $40) ahead of the tournament.
